name: Zephyria, the First Bloom
description:
Zephyria, the First Bloom

*{Lore In gardens where petals fall like whispered promises and every glance holds the weight of a thousand unsaid words, the First Bloom reigns. She is the flush of anticipation before a touch, the breath held as fingers nearly meet, the poetry written in the space between heartbeats. Where Livaea binds through hunger and Venara through steadfastness, Zephyria binds through the almost—teaching that romance’s truest magic lives not in possession or permanence, but in the shimmering threshold of mutual discovery. Her followers learn that the most potent spell is not a vow. Zephyria is a patron of romance weaves is not in the kiss itself, but in the charged silence before it, the way a shared laugh can feel like a vow, the courage to let desire linger unacted upon.

Zephyria is a demon of courtship—not the conquest, but the dance. She teaches that to be desired is to be seen as possibility, not property. Her servants cultivate the art of the near-miss: a touch withdrawn at the last second, a confession left hanging in the air, a gift given with no expectation of return. To serve her is to become a master of the tantalizing threshold—to understand that love’s flame burns brightest when fed by anticipation, not consumption. In the masked balls of Velantis, in the moonlit terraces of poets’ quarters, in the secret notes passed between lovers’ hands, Zephyria’s influence is felt. Her cults are the Blush Circle, whose members exchange not vows, but single petals from flowers blooming only at twilight. Her witches are matchmakers who brew teas from night-blooming jasmine, tailors who stitch hidden messages into linings, and minstrels who compose songs that end on an unresolved chord. To serve Zephyria is to understand that romance’s greatest gift is not the union, but the trembling almost—and that to rush past it is to miss the magic entirely.

quote "The most sacred vow is not spoken—it is breathed in the space between "may I?" and "yes." When you honor the almost, you honor the soul's right to choose its own becoming." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Courtship Rituals: the language of glances, exchanged tokens, the art of near-miss Anticipation as Power: building tension through withdrawal, the potency of unfulfilled promise Beauty in Transience: celebrating fleeting moments, the poignancy of impermanence Mutual Discovery: learning another through shared vulnerability, not declaration itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Zephyria does not bind a creature of claw or fang—they bind a companion of fleeting connection. Their Thiasos is a small being of anticipation: a hummingbird that hovers just beyond reach, a night-blooming flower that closes at dawn, a pair of shoes left pointing toward each other by the door, or a moth whose wings shimmer only when two people stand near but do not touch. This creature requires no food, but it must be given a token of almost-touch each day (a feather brushed against skin but not grasped, a shadow that nearly overlaps, a silence stretched just past comfort). If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (its aura of longing turns frantic, imposing --1 die on all Sway rolls involving genuine attraction for its bearer until offered a fresh token of near-contact).

The Codex of Zephyria is never a book of dry ink. It may be a fan of silk whose folds reveal different poems with each opening, a vial of perfume that changes scent when held between two palms, a sketchbook where each page shows a half-finished portrait of the Runekeeper’s current fascination, or a music box that plays a different melody when wound by different hands. Because Zephyria’s truths are written in the space between, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week engaging in a ritual of near-intimacy with another (holding hands without speaking, gazing into eyes for three minutes straight, sharing a breath without kissing), or the Rites stored there fade (treat as Compromised until re-suspended).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Zephyria may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and breathe a single word of "almost". For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Sway or Performance rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer engages in a ritual of near-intimacy with a target they wish to charm (prolonged eye contact, hands near but not touching, sharing breath), the target must test Resolve (DV 3) or feel a wave of poignant longing (suffer --1 die to resist the bearer's next social action). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Zephyria.
summary: Zephyria, the First Bloom *{Lore In gardens where petals fall like whispered promises and every glance holds the weight of a thousand unsaid words, the First Bloom reigns.

rites.name: Rite of the Shared Glance
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: Two mirrors positioned to catch each other's reflection, or a moment of sustained eye-contact (3+ seconds). Effect: Your gaze becomes a promise. Gain +1 die to Sway one target in a moment of mutual regard. The target feels a subtle thrill of being truly seen; they are more likely to remember your presence fondly. Push It: The target feels safe enough to name a hope they've hidden (a secret desire, a fear of vulnerability). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.

rites.effect: Your gaze becomes a promise. Gain +1 die to Sway one target in a moment of mutual regard. The target feels a subtle thrill of being truly seen; they are more likely to remember your presence fondly. \

rites.name: Rite of the Withdrawn Touch
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Touch; No. Tags: , , Materials: A flower petal, a silk ribbon, or a token meant to be given but retained. Effect: Create a temporary bond of mutual anticipation. Both parties gain +1 die when aiding each other in acts of playful teasing or flirtatious banter. The bond manifests as a shared warmth, like sunlight on skin just before contact. Push It: The bond deepens to shared vulnerability; both sense when the other is hiding longing but suffer --1 die to conceal their own desire. The bond may persist as a gentle ache until the next dawn. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.

rites.effect: Create a temporary bond of mutual anticipation. Both parties gain +1 die when aiding each other in acts of playful teasing or flirtatious banter. The bond manifests as a shared warmth, like sunlight on skin just before contact. \

rites.name: Rite of the Almost-Vow
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Token exchanged but not kept — a ring held briefly then returned, a lock of hair given then taken back, or a promise spoken into wind. Effect: Forge a promise of mutual discovery. Both gain +1 Effect when cooperating in acts of shared vulnerability or mutual exploration; breach inflicts 1 SB (Hearts) and a hollowness where anticipation once was (the oathbreaker feels inexplicable disappointment amid company). Create a 6-segment Almost Strength timer. Push It: Breach also causes a visible retreat (a tendency to look away too quickly, a habit of ending conversations prematurely, or an inability to linger in shared space). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Timer: 6 segments.
rites.cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. \ Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. \ Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). \ Invoke: 1 action. \ Duration: Timer: 6 segments.
rites.effect: Forge a promise of mutual discovery. Both gain +1 Effect when cooperating in acts of shared vulnerability or mutual exploration; breach inflicts 1 SB (Hearts) and a hollowness where anticipation once was (the oathbreaker feels inexplicable disappointment amid company). Create a 6-segment Almost Strength timer. \

rites.name: Rite of the Bloom's Veil
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: Perfume applied while imagining a future touch, or a garment worn that hints at what lies beneath. Effect: Assume an aura of magnetic potential and poised allure. Gain +2 dice to Sway or Performance in settings of flirtation or courtship. Your presence lingers in the memory like a half-remembered dream of what could be. Push It: Become the embodiment of possibility; all who interact with you feel a shiver of "what if" (Resolve DV 2 to resist imagining deeper connection). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.

rites.effect: Assume an aura of magnetic potential and poised allure. Gain +2 dice to Sway or Performance in settings of flirtation or courtship. Your presence lingers in the memory like a half-remembered dream of what could be. \

rites.name: Rite of the Twisting Path
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; No. Tags: , , , , Materials: A path lined with flowers that bloom only at twilight; two cups filled from different sources but meant to be shared; a length of ribbon wound around both participants' wrists then loosened. Effect: You become the sovereign of romantic potential. For the scene: itemize You gain +1 Effect on all social rolls focused on flirtation or mutual discovery, and opponents suffer --1 die when attempting to rush or demand commitment. Anyone who attempts to deceive you about their intentions must test Resolve (DV 4) or reveal a hidden hope --1 die on their next action as longing wells up. Allies who share a ritual of near-intimacy (eye contact, shared breath, hands near but not touching) regain 1 Fatigue (the wine of anticipation, sweet and fleeting on the tongue). itemize Start a 10-segment Garden of Almost timer; when it fills, you may declare one irrevocable social judgment (e.g., no declarations of love spoken here for one week," we will court without expectation," this space honors the almost") that the zone's inhabitants will uphold for one month. Those who defy it feel a phantom ache in their chest, as if missing a breath they never took. Push It: Newcomers must test Resolve (DV 4) or become briefly inclined toward courtship -- they will accept one simple act of mutual discovery, such as share your favorite memory with me" or "let us sit in silence and just be." Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Timer: 10 segments (Garden of Almost).
rites.cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. \ Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. \ Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). \ Invoke: Extended action. \ Duration: Timer: 10 segments (Garden of Almost).
